question_title: What will be the highest price of Brent crude oil during April 2026?
summary: Forecasting the highest Brent crude oil price during April 2026 during an unprecedented supply crisis. The Strait of Hormuz has been effectively closed since Feb 28, 2026 following US-Israel strikes on Iran that killed Supreme Leader Khamenei. Iran's new leader has vowed to keep it closed. Current Brent is ~$101, with extreme volatility (92% annualized). Already hit $119.40 intraday on March 9. Futures curve shows backwardation (May $101 → Aug $89), but this reflects expected eventual normalization, not April-specific pricing. Goldman expects Brent to average $98 in March-April but warns of $145+ in prolonged closure scenario. IEA has released 400M barrels from reserves. Monte Carlo simulation with 4-scenario mixture (20% rapid de-escalation, 30% partial reopening, 35% continued closure, 15% escalation) produces a right-skewed distribution appropriate for this max-over-period question during an active military crisis.

factors.description: Current Brent price ~$101 with May futures at $101 sets baseline. Price already exceeded $119 intraday in March, establishing a floor for what's possible.
Strait of Hormuz effectively closed since Feb 28, ~20% of global oil supply disrupted. Iran's new supreme leader vowed to keep it closed. Largest supply disruption since 1970s energy crisis.
Extreme realized volatility: 5.8% daily vol (92% annualized). Max-over-22-trading-days with this volatility pushes distribution center well above average price.
Emergency supply measures: IEA 400M barrel reserve release, US licensing Russian oil purchases, OPEC+ adding 206k bpd from April. These partially offset disruption.
Goldman Sachs base case: Hormuz recovery starts March 21, avg $98 in March-April. But 'non-linear upside' for each day beyond March 21. Full-month disruption scenario implies $145+.
Futures curve in steep backwardation (May $101 → Jun $97 → Jul $93) indicating expected price decline over time. But max during April will exceed April average.
Active military conflict with no ceasefire in sight. Iran's new leader (son of killed supreme leader) has strong personal motivation for retaliation. Risk of further escalation is material.
factors.conditional: If Hormuz closure extends through all of April

factors.description: EIA expects Brent above $95/b for next two months. This is their 'gradual easing' base case.

reasoning:
## Forecast Summary: Highest Brent Crude Price in April 2026

**Median estimate: ~$120/bbl** | Range: $103 (P10) to $158 (P90)

### Key Context
- Brent is currently ~$101 amid the largest oil supply disruption since the 1970s
- The Strait of Hormuz has been effectively closed since Feb 28 after US/Israel strikes killed Iran's Supreme Leader
- Iran's new leader has vowed to keep Hormuz closed
- Already hit $119.40 intraday on March 9
- Extreme volatility: 92% annualized

### Scenario Breakdown
| Scenario | Weight | Max Price Range |
|----------|--------|----------------|
| Rapid de-escalation | 20% | $100-115 |
| Partial reopening mid-April | 30% | $103-135 |
| Continued closure all month | 35% | $108-160 |
| Major escalation | 15% | $115-195 |

### Percentile Estimates
- **P10**: $103 (very high confidence the max exceeds this)
- **P20**: $107
- **P40**: $115
- **P60**: $125
- **P80**: $140
- **P90**: $158

The distribution is heavily right-skewed, consistent with this being a max-over-period question during an active military crisis with extreme volatility.

resolution_criteria: This question will resolve as the highest value of the [Brent Crude Oil Continuous Contract](https://www.marketwatch.com/investing/future/brn00/download-data?countrycode=uk\&mod=mw_quote_tab) during April 2026.
